REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT ANDREW DIMITRIOU'S MOTION TO VACATE DISMISSAL IS DENIED. WHILE IT IS NOT CLEAR WHY THE CASE NEEDS TO BE ACTIVE IN ORDER FOR THE PARTIES TO COMPLETE THE TERMS OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT (REPLY 4:8-9), BASED ON THE STATEMENTS MADE IN THE MOVING PAPERS THE COURT WILL ASSUME THAT DEFENDANT IS SEEKING TO VACATE THE DISMISSAL IN ORDER TO FILE A MOTION TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT WITH A CCP 664.6 PROVISION. HOWEVER, IT IS NOT NECESSARY TO VACATE THE DISMISSAL FOR DEFENDANT TO FILE SUCH MOTION. WACKEEN V. MALIS (2002) 97 CAL.APP.4TH 429; HERNANDEZ V. BOARD OF EDUCATION (2004) 126 CAL.APP.4TH 1161. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: A. PASCUAL, NOT REPORTED (D501)

The court announces the tentative ruling. Hearing is held. Argued re reinstatement. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ANDREW DIMITRIOU's Motion To Vacate Dismissal is DENIED. While it is not clear why the case needs to be active in order for the parties to complete the terms of the settlement agreement (Reply 4:8-9), based on the statements made in the moving papers the Court will assume that Defendant is seeking to vacate the dismissal in order to file a motion to enforce the settlement agreement with a CCP 664.6 provision. However, it is not necessary to vacate the dismissal for Defendant to file such motion. Wackeen v. Malis (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 429; Hernandez v. Board of Education (2004) 126 Cal.App.4th 1161. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)

Cross-Defendant Alexios Dimitrious Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or In The Alternative, Stay The Cross-Action - Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Sep-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT ALEXIOS DIMITRIOU'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, STAY THE CROSS-ACTION - FORUM NON CONVENIENS

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Denied. Cross-defendant has waived forum non conveniens grounds by not raising them at the time he filed a demurrer which was over one year ago. CCP 418.10(e)(3).

With respect to the Court acting sua sponte, Cross-Defendant failed to meet his burden of proof that Greece is a suitable alternative forum. In this regard, Cross-Complainant pled allegations relating to his discovery of facts that tolled the statute of limitations under California law. It is well established that a suit "will be entertained, no matter how inappropriate the forum may be ' if the cause of action would elsewhere be barred by the statute of limitations, unless the court is willing to accept the defendant's stipulation that he will not raise this defense in the second state, "Stangvik v Shiley (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744,752, citing to Judicial Council Comment.

Here, cross-defendant is on record as not so stipulating, instead asserting in his Reply that the court should stay the cross-action "to see if Defendant's arguments of tolling the statute of limitations would be entertained in Greece" (Reply 4: 4-5). Not only does this misstate the burden of proof in forum non conveniens motions, which, as was observed by our Supreme Court in Stangvik, is placed squarely on the moving party, it does not comport with the holding in Stangvik.

Assuming, arguendo, that Cross-Defendant had met his burden of proving that Greece is a suitable alternative forum, he also failed to meet his burden of proving that the balance of both public and private interests weigh in favor of dismissal or stay. First, because cross complainant is a California resident, there is a "strong presumption" in favor of his choice of forum. National Football League v Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. (2013) 216 Cal. App. 4th 902, 926-7,932. Second, the public factors weigh in favor of retaining the cross action here. In this regard, it was cross-defendant who initiated the underlying action by filing a complaint in this jurisdiction. A trial date is set later this month, has been pending for months and it is in the interests of judicial economy to adjudicate both the complaint and the cross complaint in one forum at one time. Third, there is a lack of competent evidence submitted by cross defendant in support of his position that private interests warrant removal. Indeed, experts will be a major part of the trial of the cross action. Noticeably cross-defendant has not attached his disclosure but did attach cross complainants' disclosure of experts that consisted of 4 experts residing in northern California and 3 who will testify here although they live in Greece.

Additionally, the Court finds prejudice to Cross-Complainant as this motion is brought three Court days before trial; after significant discovery costs were incurred; and because cross-complaint will have to travel to Greece.

For all the foregoing reasons the motion is DENIED in its entirety. Cross Defendants' request for sanctions is denied. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Angela Bradstreet, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

Nothing in the deposition questions requires plaintiff to provide a precise translation of documents. To the extent that the deposition questions call for information in documents in the Greek language, plaintiff should answer the questions to the best of his knowledge and ability of that language. Monetary sanctions are denied because plaintiff's positions do not lack substantial justification. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
